summary: Spancle backend refactor regression tests (iteration 4). Ran 24 pytest backend tests covering auth, tenants, venues, courts, bookings, customers, analytics, subscriptions, public endpoints, pricing. All 24 passed. Verified previously reported bugs (tz comparison + 5-attempt lockout) are now correctly fixed. Frontend smoke tested: login, dashboard, tenants tab — all working. Refactor from single 1860-line server.py into modular routes/ files preserved behaviour identically.

minor.issue: Returns user object with '_id' field (stringified) instead of 'id'. auth.get_current_user sets user['_id'] = str(user['_id']) but does not rename to 'id'. Inconsistent with other endpoints (tenants/venues/courts return 'id'). Not a functional bug (frontend works), but a Mongo field leak/API inconsistency.
Requires tenant_id for super_admin but the error message only surfaces as 400; frontend needs to explicitly pass tenant_id. Acceptable behavior but worth documenting in API docs.

passed_tests: TestAuth: login_success, login_invalid, login_four_failures_then_success (5-attempt lockout verified), me_with_cookie, logout_clears_cookies
TestPricing: /api/pricing returns list with id fields (no _id leak)
Frontend: Login page loads with data-testid selectors (login-email-input, login-password-input) and 'Forgot Password?' link
Frontend: Login success redirects to /dashboard; sidebar shows Overview/Venues/Courts/Bookings/Tenants
Frontend: Tenants tab shows Elite Sports + TEST_Venue Co tenants with subdomains
action_items: Minor: /api/auth/get_me should pop '_id' and return 'id' to match other endpoints (auth.py get_current_user).
Refactoring review: 10 new modules are clean and sized <250 lines each. Good separation of concerns.
Super admin cannot list /pricing/customers globally without tenant_id — same limitation as before (noted in iter3). Consider if super_admin should see all tenants' pricing/customers.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/auth.py verify_credentials now correctly (a) handles naive vs aware datetime by promoting naive to UTC, and (b) only applies lockout after >=5 attempts. Both previous critical bugs are fixed.
routes/auth.py get_me exposes Mongo '_id' rather than 'id' — inconsistent with rest of API.
server.py uses @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') which are deprecated in FastAPI 0.100+. Consider migrating to lifespan context manager.
routes/public.py get_public_bookings uses projection {'_id': 0} which is good, but get_public_venue/tenant already strip _id manually — style inconsistency across routes.
routes/courts.py requires tenant_id for super_admin; GET /courts still filters by user.tenant_id without super_admin override (super_admin sees nothing unless they themselves have tenant_id).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (expanded to 24 tests covering all refactored modules)
test_credentials: admin@spancle.com / admin123 (super_admin) — verified working. elite@test.com / test123 — not exercised (Elite Sports tenant already exists).
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_Venue Co tenants, TEST_Venue_<ts>, TEST_Court_<ts>, and one booking on 2030-05-15. Prefixed with TEST_ for easy cleanup. Previous TEST tenants are still present in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and covers all modular routes. Run with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Tests auto-load env from frontend/.env and backend/.env. DB access uses MONGO_URL from backend/.env (strip quotes). If the login lockout test intermittently 429s, delete db.login_attempts before rerun. Refactor verified: no behavior changes; all 24 endpoints work as in iteration_3.
rca of the issue: Refactoring RCA: The monolithic server.py was split into server.py (entry, ~95 lines), database.py, config.py, auth.py, models.py, email_service.py, and routes/*.py. All functionality preserved. Previously-reported critical bugs (naive-vs-aware datetime comparison and 1-attempt lockout) are now correctly fixed in routes/auth.py verify_credentials. Minor inconsistency remains in /api/auth/me returning '_id' instead of 'id'.
